store: data/eidetic.sqlite (shared-store incident window; r19's records live here)
method: deterministic, zero model calls: gold-term coverage over retrieved candidate records; verify-path span simulation via _raw_query_centered_span under both question- and gold-worded hypotheses
target: the 11 'abstained: no source entails the answer' rows (the READ-recovery class named by the phase-shift directive)

findings.detail: 10/10 READ-failure rows: the gold answer text IS present in a retrieved candidate (gold-term coverage 0.5-1.0) and that record is SHORT (2.4k-6.4k chars, below RAW_SPAN_MIN_CHARS=12000), so model NLI received the FULL gold-bearing premise and still returned no entailment. Span-centering simulation confirms even a perfectly-worded draft's span would contain the gold region (span_g coverage 1.0 on every row). The 1 retrieval row is a derived aggregation count ('Twice') that no source states verbatim - honestly unprovable by citation, the class the numeric floor already governs.

class_verdict: draft-vs-NLI: the reader's draft wording (aspect/paraphrase) fails whole-answer NLI against premises that visibly contain the answer, or NLI under-entails on multi-turn dialogue premises. NOT span selection. NOT retrieval. NOT floor calibration.
track_b_implication: The smallest-diff honest fix is DRAFT-SIDE: a verbatim claim-selection read stage (answer with the source's own sentence when the free-form draft fails verification), which makes extractive entailment fire legitimately. Loosening _extractive_entailment or any NLI floor is proof-weakening and REFUSED. This is exactly the Tier B 'read := claim_select' operator; Phase 6 implements and A/Bs it on dev, then replays these burned rows offline.
rows: see /tmp passes; per-row memory_ids withheld from committed docs (holdout hygiene), full detail reproducible via the method above
